description: Test SUT is typed as the concrete class `TeamRepository` rather than the interface `ITeamRepository`. This couples the tests to the implementation and does not verify the interface contract. All repository tests should program against the interface so that switching implementations doesn't require rewriting tests.
suggestedFix: Change the field declaration to `private readonly ITeamRepository _sut = new TeamRepository();` so the tests exercise the interface contract rather than the concrete type directly.

description: ISaveGameService and ISaveSlotService are registered as Singleton. According to the documented DI lifetime rules (Repositories: Singleton, Services: Scoped, Config: Singleton), stateful or request-scoped services should use AddScoped. Even in MAUI, if these services hold mutable state or acquire resources per operation, Singleton lifetime can cause hard-to-diagnose issues. At minimum, the decision should be documented in a comment.
suggestedFix: If these services are truly stateless (and ISaveGameService appears to be), document with a comment explaining why Singleton is chosen over Scoped. If they carry per-session state, change to AddScoped. Example: `// Singleton: stateless file I/O service; no per-request state` above each registration.

description: SaveSlotService constructor uses an optional parameter `string? customSaveDir = null` for its save directory. While this enables test injection, optional constructor parameters are a DI anti-pattern: the DI container will silently pass the default (null) and there is no way to register a different directory via normal DI configuration. This makes it impossible to configure the save directory via DI options or environment-specific config without subclassing or overriding.
suggestedFix: Introduce a dedicated options/config class (e.g., `SaveSlotOptions` with a `SaveDirectory` property) and inject it via the DI container: `services.AddSingleton(new SaveSlotOptions { SaveDirectory = ... })`. The constructor then takes `SaveSlotOptions options` instead of a nullable string. For tests, construct `SaveSlotOptions` with a temp path directly.

description: DI_ContainerRegistersServicesAsSingletons only asserts singleton lifetime for IGameStateService, IPlayerRepository, and ITeamRepository — leaving IRandomProvider, IDateTimeProvider, ISaveGameService, and ISaveSlotService unverified. If any of these were accidentally registered with the wrong lifetime in future, no test would catch it.
suggestedFix: Add singleton lifetime assertions for all 7 registered services: resolve each interface twice and assert `Should().BeSameAs()` for IRandomProvider, IDateTimeProvider, ISaveGameService, and ISaveSlotService as well.

description: TeamSpirit and Confidence are documented as 0.0–10.0 and CoachLevel as 1–8, but all properties are plain auto-properties with no range enforcement. Any caller can silently set `CoachLevel = 0` or `TeamSpirit = 99.9` and the model will accept it without complaint. Since game logic depends on these ranges (match formulas, etc.), invalid values will silently corrupt results.
suggestedFix: Add guard clauses in property setters or a `Validate()` method that throws `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` for out-of-range values. Alternatively, document clearly in code comments that validation is the responsibility of the service layer, and add service-layer guards. The current approach leaves validation entirely implicit.
